constraints.label: node, hierarchy
constraints.constraint: Hierarchy: CHASSIS contains PACKET_SWITCH, JUPITER contains SUPERBLOCK, SUPERBLOCK contains AGG_BLOCK, AGG_BLOCK contains PACKET_SWITCH, PACKET_SWITCH contains PORT

constraints.label: node, type
constraints.constraint: Each node has a 'type' attribute, in the format of EK_{TYPE}. This is important: 'type' must be a list, can include ['EK_SUPERBLOCK', 'EK_CHASSIS', 'EK_RACK', 'EK_AGG_BLOCK', 'EK_JUPITER', 'EK_PORT', 'EK_SPINEBLOCK', 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH', 'EK_CONTROL_POINT', 'EK_CONTROL_DOMAIN'].

constraints.label: capacity
constraints.constraint: When calculating capacity of a node, you need to sum the physical_capacity_bps on the PORT of each hierarchy contains in this node.

constraints.label: node, add
constraints.constraint: Adding new nodes need to consider all hierarchy. For example, adding a new switch requires adding it to the corresponding jupiter, aggregation block, and domain. The name to add on each layer can be inferred from new node name string.

constraints.label: node, attribute
constraints.constraint: Each node can have other attributes depending on its type.

constraints.label: edge, type
constraints.constraint: Each directed edge also has a 'type' attribute, include RK_CONTAINS, RK_CONTROL.

attempts.query: Add a new packet_switch 'ju1.a1.m1.s4c7' on jupiter 1, aggregation block 1, domain 1, with 5 ports, each port has physical_capacity_bps as 1000. Add node type and edges too. Return the new graph.
attempts.generated_code:
import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Parse the new node name to infer hierarchy
    new_switch_name = 'ju1.a1.m1.s4c7'
    jupiter_name = 'ju1'
    agg_block_name = 'ju1.a1.m1'
    superblock_name = 'ju1.a1'
    domain_name = 'ju1.a1.m1.s4'
    
    # Add the hierarchy nodes if they don't exist
    if jupiter_name not in graph_data:
        graph_data.add_node(jupiter_name, name=jupiter_name, type=['EK_JUPITER'])
    if superblock_name not in graph_data:
        graph_data.add_node(superblock_name, name=superblock_name, type=['EK_SUPERBLOCK'])
        graph_data.add_edge(jupiter_name, superblock_name, type='RK_CONTAINS')
    if agg_block_name not in graph_data:
        graph_data.add_node(agg_block_name, name=agg_block_name, type=['EK_AGG_BLOCK'])
        graph_data.add_edge(superblock_name, agg_block_name, type='RK_CONTAINS')
    if domain_name not in graph_data:
        graph_data.add_node(domain_name, name=domain_name, type=['EK_CHASSIS'])
        graph_data.add_edge(agg_block_name, domain_name, type='RK_CONTAINS')
    
    # Add the new packet switch
    graph_data.add_node(new_switch_name, name=new_switch_name, type=['EK_PACKET_SWITCH'])
    graph_data.add_edge(domain_name, new_switch_name, type='RK_CONTAINS')
    
    # Add ports to the new packet switch
    for port_id in range(1, 6):  # 5 ports
        port_name = f"{new_switch_name}.p{port_id}"
        graph_data.add_node(port_name, name=port_name, type=['EK_PORT'], physical_capacity_bps=1000)
        graph_data.add_edge(new_switch_name, port_name, type='RK_CONTAINS')
    
    return {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': graph_data
    }
attempts.ground_truth_code:
def ground_truth_process_graph(graph_data):
    # Create a copy of the graph
    graph_copy = graph_data.copy()

    # Create a new node for the packet_switch 'ju1.a1.m1.s4c7'
    packet_switch_node = 'ju1.a1.m1.s4c7'
    graph_copy.add_node(packet_switch_node, type=['EK_PACKET_SWITCH'])

    # Add ports to the packet_switch
    for i in range(1, 6):
        port_node = packet_switch_node + '.p' + str(i)
        graph_copy.add_node(port_node, type=['EK_PORT'], physical_capacity_bps=1000)
        graph_copy.add_edge(packet_switch_node, port_node, type='RK_CONTAINS')

    # Add the packet_switch to the corresponding jupiter, aggregation block, and domain
    jupiter_node = 'ju1'
    agg_block_node = 'ju1.a1'
    domain_node = 'ju1.a1.m1'

    if not graph_copy.has_node(jupiter_node):
        graph_copy.add_node(jupiter_node, type=['EK_JUPITER'])
    if not graph_copy.has_node(agg_block_node):
        graph_copy.add_node(agg_block_node, type=['EK_AGG_BLOCK'])
    if not graph_copy.has_node(domain_node):
        graph_copy.add_node(domain_node, type=['EK_DOMAIN'])

    graph_copy.add_edge(jupiter_node, agg_block_node, type='RK_CONTAINS')
    graph_copy.add_edge(agg_block_node, domain_node, type='RK_CONTAINS')
    graph_copy.add_edge(domain_node, packet_switch_node, type='RK_CONTAINS')

    return_object = {'type': 'graph', 'data': graph_copy}
    return return_object

import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Parse the new packet switch name to determine hierarchy
    new_switch_name = 'ju1.a1.m1.s4c7'
    jupiter_name = 'ju1'
    agg_block_name = 'ju1.a1'
    domain_name = 'ju1.a1.m1'
    ports = [f'{new_switch_name}.p{i+1}' for i in range(5)]
    port_capacity = 1000  # in bps

    # Add the hierarchy nodes if they don't exist
    if jupiter_name not in graph_data:
        graph_data.add_node(jupiter_name, name=jupiter_name, type=['EK_JUPITER'])
    if agg_block_name not in graph_data:
        graph_data.add_node(agg_block_name, name=agg_block_name, type=['EK_AGG_BLOCK'])
        graph_data.add_edge(jupiter_name, agg_block_name)
    if domain_name not in graph_data:
        graph_data.add_node(domain_name, name=domain_name, type=['EK_CONTROL_DOMAIN'])
        graph_data.add_edge(agg_block_name, domain_name)

    # Add the new packet switch
    graph_data.add_node(new_switch_name, name=new_switch_name, type=['EK_PACKET_SWITCH'])
    graph_data.add_edge(domain_name, new_switch_name)

    # Add ports to the new packet switch
    for port_name in ports:
        graph_data.add_node(port_name, name=port_name, type=['EK_PORT'], physical_capacity_bps=port_capacity)
        graph_data.add_edge(new_switch_name, port_name)

    return {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': graph_data
    }

tools.tool:
def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Filter nodes and edges
    filtered_nodes = [n for n, d in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in d['type']]
    filtered_edges = [(u, v, d) for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True) if d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and u in filtered_nodes or v in filtered_nodes]

    # Create a new graph with the filtered nodes and edges
    new_graph = nx.DiGraph()
    new_graph.add_nodes_from(filtered_nodes)
    new_graph.add_edges_from(filtered_edges)

    # Return the graph object
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': new_graph
    }
    return return_object

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Filter nodes and edges
    filtered_nodes = [n for n, d in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in d['type']]
    filtered_edges = [(u, v, d) for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True) if d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and u in filtered_nodes or v in filtered_nodes]

    # Create a new graph with the filtered nodes and edges
    new_graph = nx.DiGraph()
    new_graph.add_nodes_from(filtered_nodes)
    new_graph.add_edges_from(filtered_edges)

    # Return the graph object
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': new_graph
    }
    return return_object

attempts.generated_code:
import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify all RACK nodes
    rack_nodes = [n for n, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_RACK' in attr['type']]
    
    # Step 2: For each RACK node, count the number of CHASSIS nodes it contains
    rack_chassis_count = []
    for rack in rack_nodes:
        # Find all CHASSIS nodes directly connected to the RACK node
        chassis_nodes = [n for n in graph_data.neighbors(rack) if 'EK_CHASSIS' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type']]
        rack_chassis_count.append([graph_data.nodes[rack]['name'], len(chassis_nodes)])
    
    # Step 3: Return the result as a table
    return {'type': 'table', 'data': [['RACK', 'CHASSIS Count']] + rack_chassis_count}

attempts.generated_code:
def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify all nodes of type EK_CHASSIS within the 'ju1.a1.m1' domain
    chassis_nodes = [n for n, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True) 
                     if 'EK_CHASSIS' in attr['type'] and n.startswith('ju1.a1.m1')]

    # Step 2: Calculate the total capacity for each Chassis
    chassis_capacity = {}
    for chassis in chassis_nodes:
        packet_switches = [n for n in graph_data.neighbors(chassis) 
                           if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type']]
        total_capacity = 0
        for packet_switch in packet_switches:
            ports = [n for n in graph_data.neighbors(packet_switch) 
                     if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type']]
            for port in ports:
                total_capacity += graph_data.nodes[port].get('physical_capacity_bps', 0)
        chassis_capacity[chassis] = total_capacity

    # Step 3: Find the first and second largest Chassis by capacity
    sorted_chassis = sorted(chassis_capacity.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    top_two_chassis = sorted_chassis[:2]

    # Step 4: Convert bandwidth to Mbps and prepare the table
    result_table = [['Chassis', 'Bandwidth']]
    for chassis, capacity in top_two_chassis:
        result_table.append([chassis, capacity // 1_000_000])  # Convert bps to Mbps

    return {'type': 'table', 'data': result_table}

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Filter nodes and edges
    filtered_nodes = [n for n, d in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in d['type']]
    filtered_edges = [(u, v, d) for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True) if d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and u in filtered_nodes or v in filtered_nodes]

    # Create a new graph with the filtered nodes and edges
    new_graph = nx.DiGraph()
    new_graph.add_nodes_from(filtered_nodes)
    new_graph.add_edges_from(filtered_edges)

    # Return the graph object
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': new_graph
    }
    return return_object

attempts.generated_code:
def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify all PACKET_SWITCH nodes
    packet_switch_nodes = [n for n, d in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in d['type']]
    
    # Step 2: For each PACKET_SWITCH, find its PORT nodes using RK_CONTAINS edges
    total_capacity = 0
    total_ports = 0
    
    for switch in packet_switch_nodes:
        # Find all PORT nodes connected to this PACKET_SWITCH via RK_CONTAINS
        port_nodes = [
            v for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True)
            if u == switch and d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[v]['type']
        ]
        
        # Sum up the physical_capacity_bps for all PORT nodes
        for port in port_nodes:
            total_capacity += graph_data.nodes[port].get('physical_capacity_bps', 0)
            total_ports += 1
    
    # Step 3: Calculate the average physical_capacity_bps
    average_capacity = total_capacity / total_ports if total_ports > 0 else 0
    
    # Return the result as a string
    return_object = {
        'type': 'text',
        'data': str(average_capacity)
    }
    return return_object

    # Step 2: For each PACKET_SWITCH, find its PORT nodes
    total_capacity = 0
    total_ports = 0

        # Find all PORT nodes connected to the PACKET_SWITCH via RK_CONTAINS edges
        port_nodes = [

            if u == switch and d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[v]['type']

        for port in port_nodes:
            total_capacity += graph_data.nodes[port].get('physical_capacity_bps', 0)
            total_ports += 1
    
    # Step 3: Calculate the average physical_capacity_bps
    average_capacity = total_capacity / total_ports if total_ports > 0 else 0
    
    # Return the result as a string
    return {
        'type': 'text',
        'data': str(average_capacity)
    }

import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify the AGG_BLOCK node 'ju1.a1.m1'
    agg_block_node = 'ju1.a1.m1'
    if not ('EK_AGG_BLOCK' in graph_data.nodes[agg_block_node]['type']):
        return {'type': 'text', 'data': f"Node {agg_block_node} is not an AGG_BLOCK."}
    
    # Step 2: Find all PACKET_SWITCH nodes contained within the AGG_BLOCK node
    packet_switch_nodes = []
    for neighbor in graph_data.neighbors(agg_block_node):
        if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in graph_data.nodes[neighbor]['type']:
            # Check if the edge type is RK_CONTAINS
            if graph_data.edges[agg_block_node, neighbor].get('type') == 'RK_CONTAINS':
                packet_switch_nodes.append(neighbor)
    
    # Step 3: Calculate the average physical_capacity_bps for each PACKET_SWITCH
    packet_switch_data = []
    for packet_switch in packet_switch_nodes:
        # Find all PORT nodes contained within the PACKET_SWITCH
        ports = [n for n in graph_data.neighbors(packet_switch) if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type'] and 
                 graph_data.edges[packet_switch, n].get('type') == 'RK_CONTAINS']
        # Sum the physical_capacity_bps of all PORT nodes
        total_capacity = sum([graph_data.nodes[port].get('physical_capacity_bps', 0) for port in ports])
        # Calculate the average capacity
        average_capacity = total_capacity / len(ports) if ports else 0
        packet_switch_data.append((packet_switch, average_capacity))
    
    # Step 4: Sort the results by highest average capacity
    packet_switch_data.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    
    # Step 5: Prepare the result as a table (list of rows)
    result_table = [[ps, avg_cap] for ps, avg_cap in packet_switch_data]
    
    # Add headers to the table
    result_table.insert(0, ['Packet Switch', 'Average Capacity (bps)'])
    
    return {'type': 'table', 'data': result_table}

import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify the AGG_BLOCK node 'ju1.a1.m1'
    agg_block_node = 'ju1.a1.m1'
    
    # Step 2: Find all PACKET_SWITCH nodes contained within the AGG_BLOCK node
    packet_switches = [
        n for n in graph_data.neighbors(agg_block_node)
        if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type'] and
           any(edge_data['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' for _, v, edge_data in graph_data.edges(data=True) if _ == agg_block_node and v == n and edge_data['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS')
    ]
    
    # Step 3: Calculate the average physical_capacity_bps for each PACKET_SWITCH
    packet_switch_data = []
    for packet_switch in packet_switches:
        # Find all PORT nodes contained within the PACKET_SWITCH
        ports = [
            n for n in graph_data.neighbors(packet_switch)
            if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type'] and
               any(edge_data['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' for _, v, edge_data in graph_data.edges(data=True) if _ == packet_switch and v == n and edge_data['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS')
        ]
        
        # Calculate the total physical_capacity_bps for the PACKET_SWITCH
        total_capacity = sum(graph_data.nodes[port].get('physical_capacity_bps', 0) for port in ports)
        average_capacity = total_capacity / len(ports) if ports else 0
        
        # Append the data for this PACKET_SWITCH
        packet_switch_data.append([packet_switch, average_capacity])
    
    # Step 4: Sort the data by highest average capacity
    packet_switch_data.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    
    # Step 5: Return the data as a table
    return {
        'type': 'table',
        'data': {
            'headers': ['Packet Switch', 'Average Capacity (bps)'],
            'rows': packet_switch_data  # Ensure rows is a list of lists
        }
    }

import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify all nodes with the type attribute containing EK_PACKET_SWITCH
    packet_switch_nodes = [node for node, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in attr.get('type', [])]
    
    # Step 2: Calculate the capacity for each PACKET_SWITCH node
    packet_switch_capacities = {}
    for packet_switch in packet_switch_nodes:
        ports = [n for n in graph_data.neighbors(packet_switch) if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n].get('type', [])]
        total_capacity = sum([graph_data.nodes[port].get('physical_capacity_bps', 0) for port in ports])
        packet_switch_capacities[packet_switch] = total_capacity
    
    # Step 3: Calculate the average capacity
    average_capacity = sum(packet_switch_capacities.values()) / len(packet_switch_capacities) if packet_switch_capacities else 0
    
    # Step 4: Find all PACKET_SWITCH nodes with capacity greater than the average
    above_average_nodes = [node for node, capacity in packet_switch_capacities.items() if capacity > average_capacity]
    
    return {
        'type': 'list',
        'data': above_average_nodes
    }
attempts.ground_truth_code:
def ground_truth_process_graph(graph_data):
    # Initialize the list to store packet switch nodes and their capacities
    packet_switch_nodes = []
    total_capacity = 0

    # Iterate over all nodes in the graph
    for node in graph_data.nodes(data=True):
        # Check if the node is a packet switch
        if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in node[1]['type']:
            # Calculate the total capacity of the packet switch
            capacity = sum([graph_data.nodes[neighbor]['physical_capacity_bps'] for neighbor in graph_data.neighbors(node[0]) if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[neighbor]['type']])
            # Append the node and its capacity to the list
            packet_switch_nodes.append((node[0], capacity))
            total_capacity += capacity

    # Calculate the average capacity
    avg_capacity = total_capacity / len(packet_switch_nodes)

    # Find all packet switch nodes that have capacity more than the average
    result = [node for node, capacity in packet_switch_nodes if capacity > avg_capacity]

    # Return the result
    return_object = {'type': 'list', 'data': result}
    return return_object

import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify all nodes with type containing EK_SUPERBLOCK or EK_AGG_BLOCK
    filtered_nodes = [n for n, d in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_SUPERBLOCK' in d['type'] or 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in d['type']]
    
    # Step 2: Filter edges where both nodes are in the filtered_nodes and edge type is RK_CONTAINS
    filtered_edges = [(u, v, d) for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True) if d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and u in filtered_nodes and v in filtered_nodes]
    
    # Step 3: Create a new graph with the filtered nodes and edges
    new_graph = nx.DiGraph()
    new_graph.add_nodes_from((n, graph_data.nodes[n]) for n in filtered_nodes)
    new_graph.add_edges_from((u, v, d) for u, v, d in filtered_edges)
    
    # Return the graph object
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': new_graph
    }
    return return_object

    filtered_edges = [(u, v, d) for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True) if d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and u in filtered_nodes or v in filtered_nodes]

    # Create a new graph with the filtered nodes and edges
    new_graph = nx.DiGraph()
    new_graph.add_nodes_from(filtered_nodes)
    new_graph.add_edges_from(filtered_edges)

    # Return the graph object
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': new_graph
    }
    return return_object

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Filter nodes and edges
    filtered_nodes = [n for n, d in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in d['type']]
    filtered_edges = [(u, v, d) for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True) if d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and u in filtered_nodes or v in filtered_nodes]

    # Create a new graph with the filtered nodes and edges

attempts.generated_code:
import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify the Chassis node 'c4' and the Packet Switch node 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c4'
    chassis_node = None
    packet_switch_node = None
    
    for node, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True):
        if node == 'c4' and 'EK_CHASSIS' in attr.get('type', []):
            chassis_node = node
        if node == 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c4' and 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in attr.get('type', []):
            packet_switch_node = node
    
    if not chassis_node or not packet_switch_node:
        return {'type': 'graph', 'data': graph_data}  # Return the graph as is if nodes are not found
    
    # Step 2: Remove the directed edge of type RK_CONTAINS from 'c4' to 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c4'
    edges_to_remove = [
        (u, v) for u, v, edge_attr in graph_data.edges(data=True)
        if u == chassis_node and v == packet_switch_node and edge_attr.get('type') == 'RK_CONTAINS'
    ]
    graph_data.remove_edges_from(edges_to_remove)
    
    # Step 3: Calculate the capacity of the removed Packet Switch
    ports = [
        n for n in graph_data.neighbors(packet_switch_node)
        if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n].get('type', [])
    ]
    removed_capacity = sum(
        graph_data.nodes[port].get('physical_capacity_bps', 0) for port in ports
    )
    
    # Step 4: Remove the Packet Switch and its associated PORT nodes
    graph_data.remove_nodes_from(ports)
    graph_data.remove_node(packet_switch_node)
    
    # Step 5: Distribute the removed capacity across other Chassis nodes
    chassis_nodes = [
        n for n, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True)
        if 'EK_CHASSIS' in attr.get('type', []) and n != chassis_node
    ]
    
    if not chassis_nodes:
        return {'type': 'graph', 'data': graph_data}  # Return the graph as is if no other Chassis nodes are available
    
    capacity_per_chassis = removed_capacity / len(chassis_nodes)
    
    # Step 6: Add the removed Packet Switch to other Chassis nodes
    for target_chassis in chassis_nodes:
        # Create a new Packet Switch node for each Chassis
        new_packet_switch = f"{target_chassis}.s2c4"
        graph_data.add_node(new_packet_switch, type=['EK_PACKET_SWITCH'], packet_switch_attr={'switch_loc': target_chassis})
        graph_data.add_edge(target_chassis, new_packet_switch, type='RK_CONTAINS')
        
        # Distribute the capacity by adding PORT nodes
        num_ports = int(capacity_per_chassis)  # Assuming 1 bps per port for simplicity
        for i in range(num_ports):
            new_port = f"{new_packet_switch}.p{i+1}"
            graph_data.add_node(new_port, type=['EK_PORT'], physical_capacity_bps=1)
            graph_data.add_edge(new_packet_switch, new_port, type='RK_CONTAINS')
    
    # Step 7: Return the updated graph
    return {'type': 'graph', 'data': graph_data}

attempts.generated_code:
import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify the Chassis node 'c4' and the Packet Switch node 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c4'
    chassis_node = None
    packet_switch_node = None

    for node, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True):
        if node == 'c4' and 'EK_CHASSIS' in attr.get('type', []):
            chassis_node = node
        if node == 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c4' and 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in attr.get('type', []):
            packet_switch_node = node

    if not chassis_node or not packet_switch_node:
        return {'type': 'graph', 'data': graph_data}  # Return the original graph if nodes are not found

    # Step 2: Verify the relationship between them using the RK_CONTAINS edge type
    if not graph_data.has_edge(chassis_node, packet_switch_node) or \
       graph_data[chassis_node][packet_switch_node].get('type') != 'RK_CONTAINS':
        return {'type': 'graph', 'data': graph_data}  # Return the original graph if the relationship is invalid

    # Step 3: Remove the RK_CONTAINS edge between Chassis 'c4' and Packet Switch 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c4'
    graph_data.remove_edge(chassis_node, packet_switch_node)

    # Step 4: Calculate the capacity of each Chassis node
    chassis_capacity = {}
    for node, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True):
        if 'EK_CHASSIS' in attr.get('type', []):
            # Find all Packet Switches contained in this Chassis
            packet_switches = [
                n for n in graph_data.neighbors(node)
                if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in graph_data.nodes[n].get('type', []) and
                   graph_data[node][n].get('type') == 'RK_CONTAINS'
            ]
            # Calculate total capacity of the Chassis
            total_capacity = 0
            for ps in packet_switches:
                ports = [
                    n for n in graph_data.neighbors(ps)
                    if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n].get('type', [])
                ]
                total_capacity += sum([graph_data.nodes[port]['physical_capacity_bps'] for port in ports])
            chassis_capacity[node] = total_capacity

    # Step 5: Redistribute the load of the removed Packet Switch
    # Calculate the capacity of the removed Packet Switch
    removed_packet_switch_capacity = 0
    ports = [
        n for n in graph_data.neighbors(packet_switch_node)
        if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n].get('type', [])
    ]
    removed_packet_switch_capacity += sum([graph_data.nodes[port]['physical_capacity_bps'] for port in ports])

    # Distribute the capacity evenly among other Chassis nodes
    num_chassis = len(chassis_capacity)
    additional_capacity_per_chassis = removed_packet_switch_capacity / num_chassis

    for chassis in chassis_capacity:
        chassis_capacity[chassis] += additional_capacity_per_chassis

    # Step 6: Update the graph structure to reflect the balanced capacity
    # Assign the removed Packet Switch to the Chassis with the lowest capacity
    target_chassis = min(chassis_capacity, key=chassis_capacity.get)
    graph_data.add_edge(target_chassis, packet_switch_node, type='RK_CONTAINS')

    # Step 7: Return the updated graph
    return {'type': 'graph', 'data': graph_data}

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Define the PACKET_SWITCH nodes to process
    packet_switch_nodes = [
        'ju1.a1.m1.s2c1',
        'ju1.a1.m1.s2c2',
        'ju1.a1.m1.s2c3',
        'ju1.a1.m1.s2c4',
        'ju1.a1.m1.s2c5'
    ]
    
    # List to store the moved ports grouped by PACKET_SWITCH
    moved_ports_by_switch = []

    # Process each PACKET_SWITCH node
    for switch in packet_switch_nodes:
        if switch in graph_data.nodes and 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in graph_data.nodes[switch]['type']:
            # Get all PORT nodes connected to the PACKET_SWITCH
            port_nodes = [
                n for n in graph_data.neighbors(switch)
                if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type']
            ]
            
            # Sort the PORT nodes by their names (assuming names like p1, p2, ...)
            port_nodes.sort(key=lambda x: int(x.split('.')[-1][1:]))
            
            # Select the first five PORT nodes to remove
            ports_to_remove = port_nodes[:5]
            
            # Remove the selected PORT nodes from the graph
            for port in ports_to_remove:
                graph_data.remove_node(port)
            
            # Append the removed ports grouped by the PACKET_SWITCH to the list
            moved_ports_by_switch.append({
                'packet_switch': switch,
                'moved_ports': ports_to_remove
            })

    return {'type': 'list', 'data': moved_ports_by_switch}

attempts.query: Analyze the redundancy level of each SUPERBLOCK node, by calculating the number of alternative paths between pairs of CHASSIS nodes contains in SUPERBLOCK.
attempts.generated_code:
import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify all SUPERBLOCK nodes
    super_blocks = [node for node in graph_data.nodes if 'EK_SUPERBLOCK' in graph_data.nodes[node]['type']]
    redundancy_data = []

    # Step 2: For each SUPERBLOCK, find CHASSIS nodes it contains
    for super_block in super_blocks:
        chassis_nodes = [
            node for node in graph_data.successors(super_block)
            if 'EK_CHASSIS' in graph_data.nodes[node]['type']
        ]
        
        # Step 3: Calculate the number of alternative paths between each pair of CHASSIS nodes
        alternative_paths = {}
        for i, chassis_a in enumerate(chassis_nodes):
            for chassis_b in chassis_nodes[i+1:]:
                # Count the number of alternative paths between chassis_a and chassis_b
                paths = list(nx.all_simple_paths(graph_data, source=chassis_a, target=chassis_b))
                alternative_paths[(chassis_a, chassis_b)] = len(paths)
        
        # Step 4: Summarize redundancy level for the SUPERBLOCK
        total_paths = sum(alternative_paths.values())
        redundancy_data.append([super_block, total_paths])

    # Step 5: Return the redundancy data as a table
    return_object = {
        'type': 'table',
        'data': [['SUPERBLOCK', 'Total Alternative Paths']] + redundancy_data
    }

    return return_object

attempts.generated_code:
def process_graph(graph_data):
    import networkx as nx

    # Step 1: Identify all nodes with the type EK_SUPERBLOCK
    super_blocks = [node for node in graph_data.nodes if 'EK_SUPERBLOCK' in graph_data.nodes[node]['type']]
    redundancy_analysis = []

    # Step 2: For each SUPERBLOCK, extract CHASSIS nodes it contains using edges of type RK_CONTAINS
    for super_block in super_blocks:
        chassis_nodes = [node for node in graph_data.successors(super_block) if 'EK_CHASSIS' in graph_data.nodes[node]['type']]
        
        # Step 3: Calculate redundancy level by finding alternative paths between CHASSIS nodes
        redundancy_data = []
        for i, chassis_a in enumerate(chassis_nodes):
            for chassis_b in chassis_nodes[i+1:]:
                # Find all paths between chassis_a and chassis_b
                all_paths = list(nx.all_simple_paths(graph_data, source=chassis_a, target=chassis_b))
                redundancy_data.append({
                    'chassis_pair': (chassis_a, chassis_b),
                    'alternative_paths': len(all_paths)
                })
        
        # Step 4: Summarize redundancy level for the SUPERBLOCK
        total_redundancy = sum(data['alternative_paths'] for data in redundancy_data)
        redundancy_analysis.append([super_block, total_redundancy])

    # Step 5: Prepare the output in table format
    return_object = {
        'type': 'table',
        'data': [['SUPERBLOCK', 'Redundancy Level']] + redundancy_analysis
    }

    return return_object

def ground_truth_process_graph(graph_data):
    
    # Get all CONTROL_DOMAIN nodes
    control_nodes = [node for node, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_CONTROL_DOMAIN' in attr['type']]

    # Get all PACKET_SWITCH nodes
    packet_switch_nodes = [node for node, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in attr['type']]

    removable_nodes = []

    for packet_node in packet_switch_nodes:
        # Create a copy of the graph
        graph_copy = graph_data.copy()

        # Remove the packet node
        graph_copy.remove_node(packet_node)

        # Check if all control nodes are still connected
        if nx.is_strongly_connected(graph_copy.subgraph(control_nodes)):
            removable_nodes.append(packet_node)

    return_object = {'type': 'list', 'data': removable_nodes}

    return return_object

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Filter nodes and edges
    filtered_nodes = [n for n, d in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in d['type']]
    filtered_edges = [(u, v, d) for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True) if d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and u in filtered_nodes or v in filtered_nodes]

    # Create a new graph with the filtered nodes and edges
    new_graph = nx.DiGraph()
    new_graph.add_nodes_from(filtered_nodes)
    new_graph.add_edges_from(filtered_edges)

    # Return the graph object
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': new_graph
    }
    return return_object

attempts.query: Determine the optimal placement of a new PACKET_SWITCH node ju1.a1.m1.s2c9 with 5 PORT nodes in the format ju1.a1.m1.s2c9.p{i} (each has physical_capacity_bps 1000000000). Consider the current physical_capacity_bps distribution. The goal is to balance average capacity between AGG_BLOCK. Return the networkx graph.
attempts.generated_code:
import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify all AGG_BLOCK nodes
    agg_blocks = [n for n, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in attr['type']]
    
    # Step 2: Calculate total and average physical_capacity_bps for each AGG_BLOCK
    agg_block_capacities = {}
    for agg_block in agg_blocks:
        packet_switches = [n for n in graph_data.neighbors(agg_block) if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type']]
        total_capacity = 0
        for packet_switch in packet_switches:
            ports = [n for n in graph_data.neighbors(packet_switch) if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type']]
            for port in ports:
                total_capacity += graph_data.nodes[port]['physical_capacity_bps']
        agg_block_capacities[agg_block] = total_capacity
    
    # Step 3: Simulate adding the new PACKET_SWITCH and its PORTs to each AGG_BLOCK
    new_switch_name = 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c9'
    new_ports = [f'{new_switch_name}.p{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    new_port_capacity = 1000000000  # 1 Gbps per port
    new_switch_capacity = new_port_capacity * len(new_ports)
    
    best_agg_block = None
    best_capacity_balance = float('inf')
    updated_graph = None
    
    for agg_block in agg_blocks:
        # Clone the graph to simulate the addition
        temp_graph = graph_data.copy()
        
        # Add the new PACKET_SWITCH and its PORTs
        temp_graph.add_node(new_switch_name, type=['EK_PACKET_SWITCH'], packet_switch_attr={'switch_loc': 's2c9'})
        temp_graph.add_edge(agg_block, new_switch_name, type='RK_CONTAINS')
        for port in new_ports:
            temp_graph.add_node(port, type=['EK_PORT'], physical_capacity_bps=new_port_capacity)
            temp_graph.add_edge(new_switch_name, port, type='RK_CONTAINS')
        
        # Recalculate capacities
        temp_agg_block_capacities = agg_block_capacities.copy()
        temp_agg_block_capacities[agg_block] += new_switch_capacity
        avg_capacity = sum(temp_agg_block_capacities.values()) / len(temp_agg_block_capacities)
        capacity_balance = sum(abs(cap - avg_capacity) for cap in temp_agg_block_capacities.values())
        
        # Check if this placement is better
        if capacity_balance < best_capacity_balance:
            best_capacity_balance = capacity_balance
            best_agg_block = agg_block
            updated_graph = temp_graph
    
    # Return the updated graph with the new PACKET_SWITCH placed optimally
    return {'type': 'graph', 'data': updated_graph}
attempts.ground_truth_code:
def ground_truth_process_graph(graph_data):
    # Initialize the new PACKET_SWITCH node and its PORT nodes
    new_switch = 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c9'
    new_ports = ['ju1.a1.m1.s2c9.p{}'.format(i) for i in range(1, 6)]
    port_capacity = 1000000000

    # Add the new PACKET_SWITCH node to the graph
    graph_data.add_node(new_switch, type=['EK_PACKET_SWITCH'])

    # Add the new PORT nodes to the graph and connect them to the new PACKET_SWITCH node
    for port in new_ports:
        graph_data.add_node(port, type=['EK_PORT'], physical_capacity_bps=port_capacity)
        graph_data.add_edge(new_switch, port, type='RK_CONTAINS')

    # Calculate the average capacity of each AGG_BLOCK node
    agg_blocks = [node for node, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in attr['type']]
    agg_block_capacities = {}
    for block in agg_blocks:
        port_nodes = [node for node in graph_data.neighbors(block) if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[node]['type']]
        total_capacity = sum(graph_data.nodes[port]['physical_capacity_bps'] for port in port_nodes)
        agg_block_capacities[block] = total_capacity / len(port_nodes) if port_nodes else 0

    # Find the AGG_BLOCK node with the lowest average capacity
    min_block = min(agg_block_capacities, key=agg_block_capacities.get)

    # Connect the new PACKET_SWITCH node to the AGG_BLOCK node with the lowest average capacity
    graph_data.add_edge(min_block, new_switch, type='RK_CONTAINS')

    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': graph_data
    }

    return return_object

import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Step 1: Identify all AGG_BLOCK nodes
    agg_block_nodes = [n for n, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in attr['type']]
    
    # Step 2: Calculate the total physical_capacity_bps for each AGG_BLOCK
    agg_block_capacity = {}
    for agg_block in agg_block_nodes:
        packet_switches = [n for n in graph_data.neighbors(agg_block) if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type']]
        total_capacity = 0
        for packet_switch in packet_switches:
            ports = [n for n in graph_data.neighbors(packet_switch) if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type']]
            for port in ports:
                total_capacity += graph_data.nodes[port]['physical_capacity_bps']
        agg_block_capacity[agg_block] = total_capacity

    # Step 3: Find the AGG_BLOCK with the lowest total capacity
    target_agg_block = min(agg_block_capacity, key=agg_block_capacity.get)

    # Step 4: Add the new PACKET_SWITCH and PORT nodes to the target AGG_BLOCK
    new_packet_switch = 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c9'
    new_ports = [f'{new_packet_switch}.p{i}' for i in range(1, 6)]
    graph_data.add_node(new_packet_switch, type=['EK_PACKET_SWITCH'], packet_switch_attr={'switch_loc': 's2c9'})
    graph_data.add_edge(target_agg_block, new_packet_switch, type='RK_CONTAINS')

    for port in new_ports:
        graph_data.add_node(port, type=['EK_PORT'], physical_capacity_bps=1000000000)
        graph_data.add_edge(new_packet_switch, port, type='RK_CONTAINS')

    # Step 5: Ensure the new PACKET_SWITCH is added to the corresponding hierarchy
    # Extract hierarchy components from the new PACKET_SWITCH name
    jupiter = '.'.join(new_packet_switch.split('.')[:2])
    superblock = '.'.join(new_packet_switch.split('.')[:3])
    chassis = '.'.join(new_packet_switch.split('.')[:4])

    # Add hierarchy nodes if they don't exist
    if jupiter not in graph_data:
        graph_data.add_node(jupiter, type=['EK_JUPITER'])
    if superblock not in graph_data:
        graph_data.add_node(superblock, type=['EK_SUPERBLOCK'])
        graph_data.add_edge(jupiter, superblock, type='RK_CONTAINS')
    if chassis not in graph_data:
        graph_data.add_node(chassis, type=['EK_CHASSIS'])
        graph_data.add_edge(superblock, chassis, type='RK_CONTAINS')

    # Ensure the PACKET_SWITCH is connected to the CHASSIS
    graph_data.add_edge(chassis, new_packet_switch, type='RK_CONTAINS')

    # Return the updated graph
    return {'type': 'graph', 'data': graph_data}

def ground_truth_process_graph(graph_data):
    # Initialize the new PACKET_SWITCH node and its PORT nodes
    new_switch = 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c9'
    new_ports = ['ju1.a1.m1.s2c9.p{}'.format(i) for i in range(1, 6)]
    port_capacity = 1000000000

    # Add the new PACKET_SWITCH node to the graph
    graph_data.add_node(new_switch, type=['EK_PACKET_SWITCH'])

    # Add the new PORT nodes to the graph and connect them to the new PACKET_SWITCH node
    for port in new_ports:
        graph_data.add_node(port, type=['EK_PORT'], physical_capacity_bps=port_capacity)
        graph_data.add_edge(new_switch, port, type='RK_CONTAINS')

    # Calculate the average capacity of each AGG_BLOCK node
    agg_blocks = [node for node, attr in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in attr['type']]
    agg_block_capacities = {}
    for block in agg_blocks:
        port_nodes = [node for node in graph_data.neighbors(block) if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[node]['type']]
        total_capacity = sum(graph_data.nodes[port]['physical_capacity_bps'] for port in port_nodes)
        agg_block_capacities[block] = total_capacity / len(port_nodes) if port_nodes else 0

    # Find the AGG_BLOCK node with the lowest average capacity
    min_block = min(agg_block_capacities, key=agg_block_capacities.get)

    # Connect the new PACKET_SWITCH node to the AGG_BLOCK node with the lowest average capacity
    graph_data.add_edge(min_block, new_switch, type='RK_CONTAINS')

    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': graph_data
    }

    return return_object

attempts.generated_code:
import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Create a copy of the graph to avoid modifying the original
    updated_graph = graph_data.copy()
    
    # Define the target node name
    target_node_name = "ju1.a1.m1.s2c2.p14"
    
    # Locate the target node
    target_node = None
    for node, data in updated_graph.nodes(data=True):
        if data.get('name') == target_node_name:
            target_node = node
            break
    
    # If the target node is found, update its physical_capacity_bps
    if target_node:
        # Convert 2000 Mbps to bps
        new_capacity_bps = 2000 * 10**6  # 1 Mbps = 10^6 bps
        
        # Update the physical_capacity_bps attribute
        updated_graph.nodes[target_node]['physical_capacity_bps'] = new_capacity_bps
    
    # Return the updated graph
    return {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': updated_graph
    }

import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Create a copy of the graph to ensure the original graph is not modified
    updated_graph = graph_data.copy()
    
    # Define the target node name
    target_node_name = "ju1.a1.m1.s2c2.p14"
    
    # Convert Mbps to bps
    new_capacity_bps = 5000 * 10**6  # 5000 Mbps to bps
    
    # Locate the target node and update its physical_capacity_bps attribute
    for node, data in updated_graph.nodes(data=True):
        if data.get('name') == target_node_name:
            # Update the physical_capacity_bps attribute
            data['physical_capacity_bps'] = new_capacity_bps
            break
    
    # Return the updated graph
    return {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': updated_graph
    }

import networkx as nx

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Create a copy of the graph to ensure the original graph is not modified
    updated_graph = graph_data.copy()
    
    # Define the target node name
    target_node_name = "ju1.a1.m1.s2c2.p14"
    
    # Locate the target node in the graph
    target_node = None
    for node, data in updated_graph.nodes(data=True):
        if data.get('name') == target_node_name:
            target_node = node
            break
    
    # If the target node is not found, return the original graph
    if not target_node:
        return {'type': 'graph', 'data': updated_graph}
    
    # Update the physical_capacity_bps attribute
    # Convert 7000 Mbps to bps (1 Mbps = 1,000,000 bps)
    new_capacity_bps = 7000 * 1_000_000
    updated_graph.nodes[target_node]['physical_capacity_bps'] = new_capacity_bps
    
    # Return the updated graph
    return {'type': 'graph', 'data': updated_graph}

This is important: 'type' must be a list, can include ['EK_SUPERBLOCK', 'EK_CHASSIS', 'EK_RACK', 'EK_AGG_BLOCK', 'EK_JUPITER', 'EK_PORT', 'EK_SPINEBLOCK', 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH', 'EK_CONTROL_POINT', 'EK_CONTROL_DOMAIN'].

constraints.label: node, attribute

constraints.label: node, hierarchy

constraints.constraint: Each node can have other attributes depending on its type.

tools.tool:
def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Filter nodes and edges
    filtered_nodes = [n for n, d in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in d['type']]
    filtered_edges = [(u, v, d) for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True) if d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and u in filtered_nodes or v in filtered_nodes]

    # Create a new graph with the filtered nodes and edges
    new_graph = nx.DiGraph()
    new_graph.add_nodes_from(filtered_nodes)
    new_graph.add_edges_from(filtered_edges)

    # Return the graph object
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': new_graph
    }
    return return_object

Filter the identified CONTROL_POINT nodes to find those that also have the PACKET_SWITCH type and return the list.

step_records.summary: Locate the AGG_BLOCK type node named ju1.a1.m1 in the graph.

attempts.query: Identify all CONTROL_POINT nodes that are also PACKET_SWITCH type within the AGG_BLOCK type node ju1.a1.m1. Return a list.

constraints.label: node, type
constraints.constraint: To find node based on type, check the name and type list. For example, [node[0] == 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c1' and 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in node[1]['type']].

constraints.label: node, type
constraints.constraint: Each node has a 'type' attribute, in the format of EK_{TYPE}. This is important: 'type' must be a list, can include ['EK_SUPERBLOCK', 'EK_CHASSIS', 'EK_RACK', 'EK_AGG_BLOCK', 'EK_JUPITER', 'EK_PORT', 'EK_SPINEBLOCK', 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH', 'EK_CONTROL_POINT', 'EK_CONTROL_DOMAIN'].

constraints.label: node, attribute
constraints.constraint: packet switch nodes also have switch location attribute 'switch_loc' in node attribute 'packet_switch_attr'.

constraints.label: node, hierarchy
constraints.constraint: Hierarchy: CHASSIS contains PACKET_SWITCH, JUPITER contains SUPERBLOCK, SUPERBLOCK contains AGG_BLOCK, AGG_BLOCK contains PACKET_SWITCH, PACKET_SWITCH contains PORT

constraints.label: node, add
constraints.constraint: Adding new nodes need to consider all hierarchy. For example, adding a new switch requires adding it to the corresponding jupiter, aggregation block, and domain. The name to add on each layer can be inferred from new node name string.

constraints.label: PORT, name
constraints.constraint: For example, a PORT node name is ju1.a1.m1.s2c1.p3

constraints.label: graph, type
constraints.constraint: The graph is directed and each node has a 'name' attribute to represent itself.

constraints.label: edge, type
constraints.constraint: Each directed edge also has a 'type' attribute, include RK_CONTAINS, RK_CONTROL.

constraints.label: node, attribute
constraints.constraint: Each node can have other attributes depending on its type.

tools.tool:
def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Filter nodes and edges
    filtered_nodes = [n for n, d in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in d['type']]
    filtered_edges = [(u, v, d) for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True) if d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and u in filtered_nodes or v in filtered_nodes]

    # Create a new graph with the filtered nodes and edges
    new_graph = nx.DiGraph()
    new_graph.add_nodes_from(filtered_nodes)
    new_graph.add_edges_from(filtered_edges)

    # Return the graph object
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': new_graph
    }
    return return_object

tools.tool:
def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Filter nodes and edges
    filtered_nodes = [n for n, d in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in d['type']]
    filtered_edges = [(u, v, d) for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True) if d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and u in filtered_nodes or v in filtered_nodes]

    # Create a new graph with the filtered nodes and edges
    new_graph = nx.DiGraph()
    new_graph.add_nodes_from(filtered_nodes)
    new_graph.add_edges_from(filtered_edges)

    # Return the graph object
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': new_graph
    }
    return return_object

import networkx as nx
import copy

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Create a copy of the graph to ensure the original graph is not modified
    updated_graph = copy.deepcopy(graph_data)
    
    # Iterate through all nodes in the graph
    for node, attrs in updated_graph.nodes(data=True):
        # Check if the node is of type EK_PACKET_SWITCH
        if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in attrs.get('type', []):

def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Filter nodes and edges
    filtered_nodes = [n for n, d in graph_data.nodes(data=True) if 'EK_AGG_BLOCK' in d['type']]
    filtered_edges = [(u, v, d) for u, v, d in graph_data.edges(data=True) if d['type'] == 'RK_CONTAINS' and u in filtered_nodes or v in filtered_nodes]

    # Create a new graph with the filtered nodes and edges
    new_graph = nx.DiGraph()
    new_graph.add_nodes_from(filtered_nodes)
    new_graph.add_edges_from(filtered_edges)

    # Return the graph object
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': new_graph
    }
    return return_object

def process_graph(graph_data):
    import networkx as nx

    # Create a copy of the graph to avoid modifying the original
    updated_graph = graph_data.copy()

    # Iterate through all nodes in the graph
    for node, attrs in graph_data.nodes(data=True):
        # Check if the node is of type EK_PACKET_SWITCH
        if 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in attrs.get('type', []):
            # Check if the node has the specific attribute structure
            packet_switch_attr = attrs.get('packet_switch_attr', {})
            switch_loc = packet_switch_attr.get('switch_loc', {})
            if switch_loc.get('stage') == 3:
                # Update the stage value to 6
                updated_attrs = attrs.copy()
                updated_attrs['packet_switch_attr']['switch_loc']['stage'] = 6
                updated_graph.nodes[node].update(updated_attrs)

    # Return the updated graph
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': updated_graph
    }
    return return_object

                # Update the stage to 7

                packet_switch_attr['switch_loc'] = switch_loc
                data['packet_switch_attr'] = packet_switch_attr
    
    # Return the updated graph
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': updated_graph
    }
    return return_object

constraints.constraint: To find node based on type, check the name and type list. For example, [node[0] == 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c1' and 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in node[1]['type']].

constraints.label: PORT, name
constraints.constraint: For example, a PORT node name is ju1.a1.m1.s2c1.p3

constraints.label: node, attribute
constraints.constraint: packet switch nodes also have switch location attribute 'switch_loc' in node attribute 'packet_switch_attr'.

constraints.label: node, type
constraints.constraint: Each node has a 'type' attribute, in the format of EK_{TYPE}. This is important: 'type' must be a list, can include ['EK_SUPERBLOCK', 'EK_CHASSIS', 'EK_RACK', 'EK_AGG_BLOCK', 'EK_JUPITER', 'EK_PORT', 'EK_SPINEBLOCK', 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH', 'EK_CONTROL_POINT', 'EK_CONTROL_DOMAIN'].

constraints.label: node, add
constraints.constraint: Adding new nodes need to consider all hierarchy. For example, adding a new switch requires adding it to the corresponding jupiter, aggregation block, and domain. The name to add on each layer can be inferred from new node name string.

constraints.label: graph, type
constraints.constraint: The graph is directed and each node has a 'name' attribute to represent itself.

constraints.label: node, hierarchy
constraints.constraint: Hierarchy: CHASSIS contains PACKET_SWITCH, JUPITER contains SUPERBLOCK, SUPERBLOCK contains AGG_BLOCK, AGG_BLOCK contains PACKET_SWITCH, PACKET_SWITCH contains PORT

constraints.label: edge, add
constraints.constraint: When adding new nodes, you should also add edges based on their relationship with existing nodes.

tools.description: capacity on a packet switch example
tools.tool:
ports = [n for n in graph_data.neighbors(current_packet_switch) if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type']]
	total_capacity = sum([graph_data.nodes[port]['physical_capacity_bps'] for port in ports])

attempts.query: What is the bandwidth on packet switch ju1.a2.m1.s2c3? Output bandwidth unit should be in Mbps. Return only the number.

constraints.label: capacity
constraints.constraint: When calculating capacity of a node, you need to sum the physical_capacity_bps on the PORT of each hierarchy contains in this node.

constraints.label: node, type
constraints.constraint: To find node based on type, check the name and type list. For example, [node[0] == 'ju1.a1.m1.s2c1' and 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH' in node[1]['type']].

constraints.label: PORT, name
constraints.constraint: For example, a PORT node name is ju1.a1.m1.s2c1.p3

constraints.label: node, attribute
constraints.constraint: packet switch nodes also have switch location attribute 'switch_loc' in node attribute 'packet_switch_attr'.

constraints.label: node, type
constraints.constraint: Each node has a 'type' attribute, in the format of EK_{TYPE}. This is important: 'type' must be a list, can include ['EK_SUPERBLOCK', 'EK_CHASSIS', 'EK_RACK', 'EK_AGG_BLOCK', 'EK_JUPITER', 'EK_PORT', 'EK_SPINEBLOCK', 'EK_PACKET_SWITCH', 'EK_CONTROL_POINT', 'EK_CONTROL_DOMAIN'].

constraints.label: node, add
constraints.constraint: Adding new nodes need to consider all hierarchy. For example, adding a new switch requires adding it to the corresponding jupiter, aggregation block, and domain. The name to add on each layer can be inferred from new node name string.

constraints.label: graph, type
constraints.constraint: The graph is directed and each node has a 'name' attribute to represent itself.

constraints.label: node, hierarchy
constraints.constraint: Hierarchy: CHASSIS contains PACKET_SWITCH, JUPITER contains SUPERBLOCK, SUPERBLOCK contains AGG_BLOCK, AGG_BLOCK contains PACKET_SWITCH, PACKET_SWITCH contains PORT

constraints.label: edge, add
constraints.constraint: When adding new nodes, you should also add edges based on their relationship with existing nodes.

tools.description: capacity on a packet switch example
tools.tool:
ports = [n for n in graph_data.neighbors(current_packet_switch) if 'EK_PORT' in graph_data.nodes[n]['type']]
	total_capacity = sum([graph_data.nodes[port]['physical_capacity_bps'] for port in ports])

Retrieve the bandwidth attribute of the identified node.
Convert the bandwidth value to Mbps if necessary and return the number.
